name: Adoption OS Agentic Build Plan
hardConstraint: The artifact must live inside the Glean product as a single-file Glean-ready HTML artifact.
globalGuardrails: Do not introduce live network calls from the artifact.
Do not use browser storage APIs.
Do not add iframes, external scripts, or external CSS imports.
Keep the V1 data layer, AI Fluency placeholder, and source taxonomy intact.

roles.controller: Main Codex agent. Owns sequencing, integration, validation, and final user-facing summary.
roles.implementer: Worker sub-agent. Owns a specific slice and writes only within the declared write scope.
roles.specReviewer: Reviewer sub-agent. Checks slice output against scope and acceptance criteria.
roles.runtimeReviewer: Reviewer sub-agent. Checks Glean runtime safety and repo validation risks.

slices.title: Roster Verification
slices.goal: Let an AIOM confirm or edit their suggested client roster before portfolio intelligence loads.
requirements: Show suggested clients from seeded Staffing Sheet / Salesforce-style roster data.
Support select all, individual selection, and adding a missing client.
Only verified clients should appear in the portfolio view after confirmation.
Expose verification state in the artifact data model.
acceptanceCriteria: AIOM can verify all suggested clients in one action.
AIOM can deselect clients that are not theirs.
AIOM can add a missing client with a visible aggregation status.
Portfolio table respects the verified roster.
npm run check passes.

slices.title: Aggregation Status Layer
slices.goal: Show what source data is available, missing, or needs verification for each verified account.
requirements: Represent Salesforce, AIOM Staffing Sheet, Rocketlane, Sigma, Slack, Gong/meetings, Docs/Drive, and Prism status.
Show confidence reason and supporting sources in a human-readable way.
Do not imply live data when using seeded data.
acceptanceCriteria: Each account exposes source coverage by category.
Missing sources are visible.
Confidence reason is visible near the recommendation.
npm run check passes.

slices.title: Strategic Action Module
slices.goal: Make strategic action, rationale, best next step, owner, target audience, and recommended resource obvious on the first screen.
requirements: Render strategicAction, strategicRationale, bestNextStep, decisionOwner, targetFunction, primaryFrictionType, recommendedToolOrAgent, recommendedAsset, and capturePrompt.
Keep the experience practical for an AIOM and tied to Adoption OS.
Avoid marketing copy or abstract dashboard language.
acceptanceCriteria: An AIOM can answer what to do next and why from the first screen.
Recommended Glean resource is visible next to the next step.
npm run check passes.

slices.title: Adoption OS Phase Backbone
slices.goal: Make the Adoption OS framework explicit as the operating backbone of the artifact.
requirements: Show current phase, phase objective, current milestone, open activities, and phase-relevant assets.
Preserve phase-aware templates and tools.
Keep Monday/weekly priority as a summary layer, not the operating model.
acceptanceCriteria: Overview is visibly phase-led.
Phase objective and open activities are inspectable.
npm run check passes.

slices.title: Recommended Resource Launcher
slices.goal: Map strategic actions to the best Glean agent, skill, tool, or asset.
requirements: Keep existing tools.
Add placeholders or entries for AIOM Account Ramp Companion, Meeting Summarizer + Follow Up, and Optimal Agent Use Case Recommender if URLs are known or can be safely represented as copy prompts.
Launch/copy behavior must remain explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Every strategic action has a recommended Glean resource.
Missing direct URLs degrade to copyable prompt behavior.
npm run check passes.

slices.title: Prism Capture Loop
slices.goal: Turn Prism capture into the memory loop for strategic action outcomes.
requirements: Capture action taken, action outcome, remaining blocker, owner/date commitments, what worked, what did not work, and recommended follow-up.
Keep capture session-local unless a Glean-native persistence path is explicitly added.
Make it clear what should be copied or saved into Prism.
acceptanceCriteria: Capture form reflects the strategic action model.
AIOM can capture whether the recommendation worked.
npm run check passes.

slices.title: Glean Runtime Readiness
slices.goal: Keep the final artifact safe to place back inside Glean.
requirements: Preserve single-file HTML output.
Keep runtime restrictions encoded in validation.
Document placement steps and smoke test steps.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run check passes.
Runtime docs include the current smoke test.
Validation catches Glean-breaking patterns.

slices.title: In-Glean Smoke Test
slices.goal: Document the final in-product test path for the user or a Glean operator.
requirements: List the exact checks to perform after placing dist/glean_adoption_os.html into Glean.
Include roster verification, account clickthrough, strategic action, resource launcher, Prism capture, and export behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Smoke test checklist is documented.
No code runtime changes are needed for this slice unless a defect is found.
npm run check passes.
